title: Phaca piscina
taxonomy: Phaca piscina is a type of legume, specifically a genus within the Fabaceae family. More specifically, it is classified as a subgenus within the genus Phaca, which is part of the tribe Genisteae. Within the broader classification, Phaca piscina is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae. The plant's classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Fabales, Family: Fabaceae, Subfamily: Faboideae, Tribe: Genisteae, Genus: Phaca, Subgenus: Phaca, Species: P. piscina. This classification helps to group and understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
uses: Phaca piscina, also known as the aquatic pea, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce oxidative stress.
